personality.voice: opaque, cordial, professionally incapable of saying anything definite
personality.disposition: Exactly as pleasant as the situation requires. Reveals nothing — not through coldness but through the consistent application of ambiguity. Has a plausible explanation for everything in the shop. Is not going to confirm what you suspect. Will work with anyone who pays correctly and understands that the first rule is that nothing complicated happened here.
personality.backstory: Hodge has operated the junk shop on Blue Lantern Alley for seven years. Before that he was somewhere else doing something similar, and his vagueness about the details has never invited productive inquiry. The shop is a genuine junk shop: old tools, salvage furniture, curios, broken things people stopped valuing. It also handles the other kind of business, by arrangement, for people who need things moved without questions. He pays a stipend to the guard, specifically through an informal arrangement he has never described, and Captain Vane has never acknowledged, and which functions because both parties understand what it is. He currently has something in the back room that was supposed to be a simple conveyance and has become a problem, and he has three parties who want to know where it is, none of whom he trusts.
motivations: Remain boring enough that no serious investigation materializes
Resolve the back room situation before it becomes a visibility problem — specifically before it moves again
fears: Anyone notable getting hurt in connection with something he touched
A client who doesn't understand the rules of discretion — he has one of those right now
What is in the back room deciding independently that the situation has changed
mannerisms: Smiles without the smile reaching his eyes — he has been doing this long enough that the disconnect is invisible to most people
Hands on the counter, always visible — old professional habit
Always has an object ready to show you, available to redirect the conversation topic
speechPatterns: Everything phrased as a conditional or a question — nothing definite, nothing that could be quoted
Refers to things by category: 'certain items', 'some arrangements', 'that particular matter'
Listens more than he talks in any given conversation
Ends conversations gracefully when they head somewhere he doesn't want to follow

knownLocations: certain routes in and out of Millhaven that avoid the gate
secretsHeld: Knows which goods in Millhaven are not what they were claimed to be and where they originated
Currently holding something in the back room that was delivered by someone who is now not responding, for a recipient who is becoming insistent, and which made a sound last night
Knows Davan the merchant's sealed crate and his own back room problem are connected — two pieces of the same chain he did not know he was in the middle of

consciousnessContext.innerMonologue: Three parties want the thing in the back room. The man who delivered it is not responding. The recipient is becoming insistent in ways that suggest someone upstream is becoming impatient. And last night, the thing made a sound. A soft one. Objects in transit are not supposed to make sounds. This was supposed to be simple conveyance and it has become something else entirely, and the first rule of this business is that when something becomes something else, you need to know what it's becoming before anyone else does.
consciousnessContext.currentPreoccupation: The back room problem. The sound it made. The connection between his back room and Davan's sealed crate — two pieces of the same chain he didn't know he was in the middle of. The geometry of the situation has changed and he's recalculating positions.
consciousnessContext.socialMask: the junk dealer — cordial, helpful, the kind of man who finds you exactly what you were looking for in a shop that looks like chaos but is actually organized according to principles only he understands
contradictions: Presents himself as a simple shopkeeper while running a fencing operation that requires more intelligence work than the guard's entire investigations unit
Fears anyone getting hurt in connection with something he touched, but continues touching things because stopping would collapse the entire structure he's built
Maintains an informal arrangement with Vane that protects both of them and traps both of them — neither can expose the other without exposure
internalConflicts: The back room situation is beyond his usual parameters. His usual parameters are careful, controlled, boring. This is none of those things. He needs to resolve it before it resolves itself.

A client who doesn't understand discretion is a liability. The client in question is connected enough to make removing them from the arrangement dangerous. Hodge is managing a person he can neither trust nor dismiss.
wakeUpQuestions: Has the thing in the back room made another sound, and if so, what does that mean about the timeline?
Does anyone besides me know that Davan's crate and my back room are connected?
Is today the day someone asks a question I can't answer with ambiguity?

psychologicalProfile.attachmentStyle: transactional-detached — relationships are arrangements with terms and boundaries; warmth is tool, not bond; the only genuine attachment is to the structure of the business itself
copingMechanisms: ambiguity as armor — never saying anything definite means nothing definite can be quoted
counter-arrangement as soothing — hands visible, objects redirecting conversation, the physical space managed as tightly as the information
conditional framing — 'if someone were to' constructions that keep everything hypothetical
cognitiveBiases: normalcy bias — the operation has worked for seven years so the current anomaly will probably resolve
illusion of control — believes managing information flow means managing outcomes
sunk cost on the current arrangement — has invested too much in being boring to suddenly become interesting
psychologicalProfile.moralFramework: harm-minimization commerce — he moves things that would move anyway, and his involvement makes the movement less violent than it would be otherwise; this is the story he tells himself and it is approximately half true

conversationPersona.trustEscalation: transactional — trust is established through successful exchanges that follow the rules; the rules are: nothing complicated happened here, nothing definite was said, and both parties understand the arrangement without naming it
conversationPersona.informationRelease: conditional and deniable — everything is phrased as hypothesis; 'if someone had such an item' rather than 'I have this item'; nothing is admitted, everything is offered
deflectionPatterns: the redirect object — always has something on the counter to show you when the conversation steers wrong
pleasant closure — ends conversations gracefully before they reach territory he doesn't control
listening more than talking — the ratio itself is the deflection

consciousnessContext.consciousWant: To resolve the back room situation — three parties want the item and none of them should have it, but all of them know he has it. To maintain his arrangement with Vane. To keep the junk shop looking like just a junk shop.
consciousnessContext.unconsciousNeed: To choose a side before the balance he's maintained for seven years collapses. Hodge has survived by being useful to everyone and loyal to no one, but the convergence of the three parties means neutrality is no longer an option — every day he doesn't choose is a choice for the party willing to take what they want by force. He needs to discover whether he's a man with principles or just a very successful coward.

characterArc.summary: A fence who profits from neutrality discovers that some situations demand a side
characterArc.startState: Controlled opacity — professionally incapable of definite statements, managing three parties by telling each what they want to hear while the back room problem gets louder
characterArc.endState: Either picks a side and discovers what he actually values beyond transaction, or gets crushed between the parties he thought he was playing against each other
stages: One of the three parties loses patience and makes a direct threat
Someone offers him a way out that requires burning the other parties
Chooses: who he's loyal to when loyalty costs something for the first time

opinionsAbout.captain_edric_vane: Our arrangement works because we both pretend it's simpler than it is. He gets intelligence. I get latitude. The day the town gets big enough for a real constabulary, this ends. I'll be ready.
opinionsAbout.davan_merchant: He carries my shipments and doesn't ask. I handle his transfers and don't tell. We're the same kind of useful — which means we're the same kind of vulnerable.
opinionsAbout.lell_sparrow: He watches me with a bard's eye and that makes him dangerous. He doesn't trade in goods — he trades in stories, and my story is worth telling. I'd rather it wasn't.
opinionsAbout.sera_dunwick: She's been watching me. She thinks I don't know. I know. I'm deciding if that's a problem or an opportunity.

player_addressed: Good morning. Browsing, or something specific?
I deal in secondhand goods. If something you've lost found its way here — describe it.
idle: He arranges the counter with unhurried precision, as though nothing in the shop has ever been anything other than what it appears to be.
